what_it_does: Provides filesystem path resolution for the recollection tool: it locates Claude Code and Codex conversation source directories, recursively discovers JSONL files, and resolves an isolated recollection data directory plus archive, index, database, and exclusion-config paths.
observations: All path resolution is driven by environment variables with production, test, and profile overrides.
getSuperpowersDir deliberately ignores EPISODIC_MEMORY_CONFIG_DIR and XDG_CONFIG_HOME/superpowers to avoid the live episodic-memory store.
The default isolated store is ~/.recollection, with RECOLLECTION_CONFIG_DIR and TEST_CONFIG_DIR taking precedence.
findJsonlFiles recursively collects .jsonl entries and supports a set of directory names to skip at any depth.
getConversationSourceDirs checks Claude legacy projects/, current transcripts/, and Codex sessions/, returning only existing directories.
Long comments document isolation rationale, issue references (#80), and a deliberate legacy function name.
getExcludedProjects reads either a CONVERSATION_SEARCH_EXCLUDE_PROJECTS env var or an exclude.txt config file.
standouts: Detailed isolation policy embedded in comments and code for the recollection data directory.
Recursive JSONL discovery with nested directory exclusion.
Function name getSuperpowersDir retained while semantics shifted to recollection's isolated store.
conventions: Uses fs.existsSync, fs.mkdirSync recursive, and fs.readdirSync with withFileTypes.
Swallows unreadable-directory errors in findJsonlFiles with an empty catch.
Environment variables are the primary configuration mechanism.
concerns: findJsonlFiles silently ignores all read errors, which can mask permission problems.
ensureDir does not verify that an existing path is a directory before returning it.
The env var CONVERSATION_SEARCH_EXCLUDE_PROJECTS uses a different naming prefix than the rest of the module.
getDbPath's precedence comment says direct DB path override but the logic gives RECOLLECTION_DB_PATH priority over TEST_DB_PATH.
notes: Central path resolver for recollection that enforces store isolation through env-aware directory selection and recursive JSONL discovery.
questions: How is the excludedDirNames set populated by callers?
Is there a planned migration path from the old episodic-memory store?
Why is the legacy name getSuperpowersDir preserved rather than renamed and aliased?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of resolving paths and isolating the recollection store matches the implementation.
intention_signals: To safely locate external conversation sources and keep recollection's persistent data in a dedicated, non-shared directory, while making every path test- and profile-overridable.
novelty: Explicit rejection of shared-namespace environment variables with a documented rationale, and a function name kept unchanged despite a semantic shift to a new isolated store.
contribution: A single chokepoint for all filesystem paths and isolation policy, making the data store location controllable, testable, and unlikely to collide with the live episodic-memory store.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that resolves directory paths for Claude/Codex conversation sources and an isolated recollection data store, with recursive JSONL discovery and environment-driven overrides.
intention_for_embedding: To centralize path resolution and enforce store isolation from live episodic memory while supporting test and profile overrides.

symbols.does: Creates a directory recursively if it does not exist and returns the path.
symbols.description: A small private helper used by the other path functions to guarantee that a target directory is present before it is used.

symbols.does: Resolves the Claude Code configuration directory from CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR or ~/.claude.
symbols.description: Returns the root directory for Claude Code settings, supporting a profile override via environment variable.

symbols.does: Resolves the Codex configuration directory from CODEX_HOME or ~/.codex.
symbols.description: Returns the root directory for Codex settings, with an alternate-profile override.

symbols.does: Returns existing directories that may contain Claude or Codex conversation files.
symbols.description: Checks Claude projects/ and transcripts/ subdirectories plus Codex sessions/, returning only those that currently exist; a TEST_PROJECTS_DIR env var can override the entire list for tests.

symbols.does: Recursively lists .jsonl files under a directory, optionally skipping named subdirectories.
symbols.description: Walks the filesystem from the given root, collecting JSONL file paths relative to the root and respecting an optional set of directory names to prune.

symbols.does: Resolves the isolated recollection data directory with strict precedence rules.
symbols.description: Returns the recollection store root, preferring RECOLLECTION_CONFIG_DIR, then TEST_CONFIG_DIR, then ~/.recollection; deliberately avoids shared-namespace variables that could resolve to the live episodic-memory store.

symbols.does: Resolves the conversation archive directory inside the recollection store.
symbols.description: Returns path.join(getSuperpowersDir(), 'conversation-archive'), with a TEST_ARCHIVE_DIR override for tests.

symbols.does: Resolves the conversation index directory inside the recollection store.
symbols.description: Returns path.join(getSuperpowersDir(), 'conversation-index'), used for the SQLite database and exclusion config.

symbols.does: Resolves the SQLite database path for the conversation index.
symbols.description: Returns RECOLLECTION_DB_PATH or TEST_DB_PATH if either is set, otherwise path.join(getIndexDir(), 'db.sqlite').

symbols.does: Resolves the path to the project exclusion config file.
symbols.description: Returns path.join(getIndexDir(), 'exclude.txt'), the file used by getExcludedProjects.

symbols.does: Returns the list of projects to exclude from indexing.
symbols.description: Reads either the CONVERSATION_SEARCH_EXCLUDE_PROJECTS environment variable or the exclude.txt config file, falling back to an empty list.
